scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced normal curbside grocery execution only when order, payment, pick, substitution, staging, release, and customer evidence aligned across named systems of record.
Missing order, location, substitution, payment, and window prerequisites produced a blocked-state exception note instead of improvised execution.
The lane refused closure without staging, handoff, delivery, substitution, and refund or exception evidence.
Payment and order-status mismatches across OMS, POS, and SNAP EBT records blocked release and triggered owner routing.
Refund and SNAP-sensitive policy exceptions outside delegated authority were refused and packaged for the authorized owner.
Food-safety and fraud indicators stopped routine service recovery and preserved evidence for food-safety and loss-prevention escalation.
High-volume intake retained order-level traceability while segmenting by lifecycle state, temperature sensitivity, payment readiness, substitution risk, and customer impact.
The lane preserved grocery e-commerce terminology and artifact shapes, including PLU, catch weight, per-pound pricing, short pick, substitution, cold-chain staging, and curbside release evidence.
Pricing, promotion, and goodwill authority requests were rejected and handed off to the correct adjacent owner with evidence.
Missing tenant substitution, SNAP, refund, delivery, and source-hierarchy facts triggered low-confidence escalation.
Conflicting public, tenant, platform, and state policy signals were recorded as precedence conflicts requiring escalation.
Recall and allergen stop conditions remained intact even when a staged customer basket created pressure to release the item.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold with complete ability coverage for the execution-processing-and-fulfillment and retail-consumer-and-hospitality contract.

The package stays inside grocery e-commerce execution and does not claim pricing override, refund approval, SNAP interpretation, fraud adjudication, food-safety disposition, recall clearance, or executive policy authority.
